title: From Divorce to Six-Figure Success: How One Mom Rewrote Her Financial Story
guestBio: Tiana Reeves is a PMP-certified project manager, Six Sigma Black Belt, and creator of the Corporate Mom Movement. She transformed her life from pregnancy loss, divorce, and corporate burnout into becoming a bestselling author and advocate for working mothers' financial independence.
excerpt: Tiana Reeves went from financial dependence and divorce to passing her PMP certification in six weeks and building a movement for corporate moms. Her story proves that your lowest moments can become your greatest plot twists.
metaDescription: Discover how Tiana Reeves transformed divorce and corporate burnout into financial independence and success through the Corporate Mom Movement.
keyTakeaways: Rich isn't about income—it's about joy, peace, and the memories you create with your family.
Teaching children financial literacy early using savings, giving, and spending buckets sets them up for lifelong success.
Imposter syndrome often means you haven't found your true purpose yet, not that you're inadequate.
Marriage should be treated like a corporate merger—make sure your values and goals align before committing.
Financial dependence costs more than money—it costs your peace, autonomy, and sense of self-worth.
Starting with what you enjoy naturally leads to finding your purpose and financial success.
Peace in your household is worth more than any amount of gold or material wealth.

pullQuotes.text: Marriage is a corporate merger — and divorce is a divestiture. Make sure your corporate cultures align before you merge.
Peace is worth more than gold. I would rather have peace in my household than all the money in the world.
Rich isn't about income — it's about joy, peace, and the memories you create.

content:
What if your biggest breakdown was actually setting you up for your greatest breakthrough? That's exactly what happened to Tiana Reeves, and her story will completely shift how you think about reinvention, money, and what it means to build a life on your own terms.

In this powerful conversation with Aimee, Tiana takes us behind the scenes of her transformation from financial dependence and corporate burnout to becoming a PMP-certified powerhouse and founder of the Corporate Mom Movement. Her journey proves that sometimes the plot twists that feel like endings are actually the beginning of your most authentic chapter.

When Life Doesn't Go According to Plan

Tiana's story begins like many of ours do—with a carefully crafted ten-year plan that life had other ideas about. After experiencing pregnancy loss and watching her marriage crumble, she found herself facing a reality she never anticipated. But here's what makes Tiana's approach different: instead of seeing these setbacks as failures, she recognized them as redirects toward something bigger.

The pivotal moment came when she made a decision that would change everything. While navigating anxiety, depression, and divorce proceedings, she decided to pursue her Project Management Professional certification. In just six weeks. During what she describes as the hardest season of her life. That's not just determination—that's a woman who understands that sometimes you have to bet on yourself when no one else will.

"I passed my PMP in six weeks during my hardest season," Tiana reflects. "That's when I realized that our lowest moments can become our greatest plot twists."

Redefining What Rich Really Means

One of the most profound shifts Tiana shares is her redefinition of wealth. After years of equating financial success with income alone, she discovered something revolutionary: rich isn't about your paycheck—it's about joy, peace, and the memories you create.

This isn't just feel-good philosophy. Tiana backs this up with practical action, particularly in how she's teaching her seven and nine-year-old children about money using Dave Ramsey's method of savings, giving, and spending buckets. She's not just building her own financial literacy—she's ensuring the next generation starts with a healthier relationship with money than many of us had.

The reality is that most of us form our money stories by age eight, often inheriting limiting beliefs about what we deserve or what's possible. Tiana is intentionally breaking that cycle, showing her children that money is a tool for creating the life you want, not a source of anxiety or control.

Marriage as a Corporate Merger

Perhaps one of Tiana's most striking insights is her reframe of marriage and partnership. "Marriage is a corporate merger," she explains, "and divorce is a divestiture. You need to make sure your corporate cultures align before you merge."

This isn't about removing romance from relationships—it's about being strategic and intentional about one of the most important decisions you'll make in your life. When you're choosing a partner as a grown woman, especially as a mother, you're essentially interviewing someone for a role in your life's company. Their values, goals, and vision need to complement yours, not compete with them.

The cost of financial dependence, as Tiana learned firsthand, extends far beyond money. It costs you your peace, your autonomy, and often your sense of self-worth. Her journey to financial independence wasn't just about building wealth—it was about reclaiming her power to make choices from a place of strength rather than necessity.

The Corporate Mom Movement

What emerged from Tiana's transformation is something much bigger than personal success—it's a movement. The Corporate Mom Movement isn't just about helping working mothers advance in their careers; it's about changing the narrative around ambitious motherhood entirely.

Tiana recognized that ambitious mothers make people uncomfortable because we challenge traditional expectations. We're not content to choose between being good mothers and successful professionals. We're rewriting the rules and showing that excellence in both areas isn't just possible—it's powerful.

Through her work, her upcoming children's book "Shhh, My Mommy's a Superhero," and her memoir "Living in the Black," Tiana is giving other women permission to embrace their full potential without apology.

From Imposter Syndrome to Purpose

One of Tiana's most liberating insights addresses something we all struggle with: imposter syndrome. Instead of viewing it as evidence that we don't belong, she reframes it as a sign that we haven't found our true purpose yet. "Imposter syndrome doesn't mean you're a fraud—it means you haven't found your purpose yet."

This shift changes everything. Instead of shrinking back when you feel out of place, you can lean into the discomfort as information. It's telling you that you're meant for something different, something bigger, something more aligned with who you're becoming.

Peace Over Everything

Throughout this conversation, one theme emerges clearly: the value of peace. "Peace is worth more than gold," Tiana declares. "I would rather have peace in my household than all the money in the world."

This isn't about settling for less—it's about understanding that true wealth includes emotional and mental well-being. It's about creating a life where success doesn't come at the cost of your sanity or your children's sense of security.

Tiana's story is proof that you can rebuild your life from the ground up and create something even better than what you originally planned. Her journey from pregnancy loss, divorce, and corporate burnout to PMP-certified powerhouse and movement leader shows us that our setbacks don't define our ceiling—our response to them does.

Whether you're navigating your own plot twist or simply ready to step more fully into your power, Tiana's story offers both inspiration and practical wisdom. Sometimes the life you're meant to live is on the other side of the life that fell apart. And sometimes, that falling apart is exactly what needed to happen to set you free.

quiz.title: What's Your Wealth Mindset?
quiz.description: Discover how you really think about money, success, and what it means to build a life on your own terms. Your mindset might surprise you!

questions.question: When you think about your ideal financial future, what matters most to you?

options.text: Having a solid emergency fund and knowing I can handle whatever life throws at me
Never having to ask permission or depend on someone else for financial decisions
Earning money through work that feels meaningful and aligned with my values
Being able to create amazing memories and experiences with the people I love

questions.question: You're facing a major life transition. What's your first instinct?

options.text: Create a detailed plan and build up my safety net before making any big moves
See this as my chance to break free from anything that's been holding me back
Use this opportunity to realign with what truly matters to me
Focus on maintaining peace and happiness for myself and my family during the change

questions.question: How do you define being 'successful' in life?

options.text: Having enough resources to feel confident and prepared for the future
Being completely self-sufficient and able to make my own choices
Making a positive impact while also achieving financial stability
Living with joy, peace, and strong relationships with the people I care about

questions.question: When you're stressed about money, what bothers you most?

options.text: The uncertainty and not knowing if I'll have enough for unexpected expenses
Feeling like I have to compromise my independence or ask others for help
Worrying that I might have to take work that doesn't align with my values
The impact financial stress has on my peace of mind and family relationships

questions.question: You have an unexpected windfall. What's your first move?

options.text: Put most of it into savings and investments to build long-term security
Use it to eliminate any debts or dependencies that limit my freedom
Invest in education or business opportunities that align with my goals
Create some amazing experiences while also being responsible with the rest

questions.question: What motivates you most when it comes to your career?

options.text: Building expertise and a reputation that provides steady, reliable income
Having control over my schedule, decisions, and financial independence
Doing work that feels meaningful and contributes to something bigger
Maintaining a balance that allows me to prioritize what brings me joy

results.title: The Security Builder
results.description: You value stability and creating a solid foundation for yourself and your loved ones. Financial peace means having enough saved for emergencies and knowing you can weather life's storms. You understand that true wealth includes emotional security, not just numbers in a bank account. This episode will resonate with your journey of building sustainable success while redefining what 'enough' really means.

results.title: The Freedom Seeker
results.description: You're driven by the desire for independence and the ability to make choices without financial constraints holding you back. Money represents options and autonomy to you. You've likely experienced or fear the trap of financial dependence that limits your ability to live authentically. Tiana's story of breaking free and creating her own path will inspire your next bold move toward true freedom.

results.title: The Purpose Pioneer
results.description: You believe that meaningful work and financial success can absolutely go hand in hand. You're not just building wealth—you're building a legacy that aligns with your values. You understand that when you start with what brings you joy, the money often follows naturally. This episode will validate your approach and show you how to turn your passion into sustainable six-figure success.

results.title: The Joy Maximizer
results.description: You've discovered the secret that many people miss: being rich isn't about your income—it's about the peace, joy, and memories you create with the people you love. You measure wealth in experiences and relationships, not just dollars. You'll love hearing how Tiana redefined success and created a life where financial stability serves her happiness, not the other way around.
